flashcards.q: What is the earliest kind of print technology called?
Name the three East Asian countries where hand printing first developed.
What was the 'accordion book' in China?
flashcards.a: A book made of thin, porous paper, folded and stitched at the side because both sides could not be printed.

flashcards.q: Define 'Calligraphy'.
flashcards.a: The art of beautiful and stylised writing.

flashcards.q: When was the Buddhist 'Diamond Sutra' printed in Japan?
What kind of world did the art form 'ukiyo' depict?
flashcards.a: The 'floating world' or ordinary human experiences, especially urban ones in 18th-century Japan.

flashcards.q: Who brought woodblock printing knowledge to Europe in 1295?
What is 'Vellum'?
flashcards.a: A high-quality parchment made from the skin of animals.

flashcards.q: In which city did Johann Gutenberg develop the printing press in the 1430s?
What was the first book printed on Gutenberg's mechanical press?
How many copies of the Bible were printed in Gutenberg's first production?
Define 'Platen' in letterpress printing.
flashcards.a: A board pressed onto the back of the paper to get the impression from the type.

flashcards.q: Who was a 'Compositor'?
flashcards.a: The person who composed the text for printing.

flashcards.q: What was a 'Galley' in a printer’s workshop?
flashcards.a: A metal frame in which types were laid and the text composed.

flashcards.q: Define 'Ballad'.
flashcards.a: A historical account or folk tale in verse, usually sung or recited.

flashcards.q: What were 'Taverns' used for, besides drinking?
flashcards.a: Gathering places to exchange news and meet friends.

flashcards.q: Who wrote 'Ninety Five Theses' initiating the Protestant Reformation?
When did the Roman Catholic Church begin maintaining the 'Index of Prohibited Books'?
What were 'penny chapbooks' in England?
flashcards.a: Pocket-size books sold by travelling pedlars (chapmen) for a penny.

flashcards.q: Who declared the printing press as the 'most powerful engine of progress'?
Name the 19th-century New York inventor who perfected the power-driven cylindrical press.
What was the first book printed in India, by whom, and when?
flashcards.a: A Konkani tract by Jesuit priests in the mid-16th century.

flashcards.q: Who started the first independent English weekly in India, the 'Bengal Gazette'?
Name the person who brought out the first weekly in an Indian language, the 'Bengal Gazette'.
Which 1878 Act gave the government extensive rights to censor vernacular newspapers?
Who published 'Istri Dharm Vichar' in Punjab?
What is a 'Fatwa'?
flashcards.a: A legal pronouncement on Islamic law, usually given by a mufti.
